title: Classes, Private State, Inheritance, and Composition · Transfer Build
summary: Build maintainable scheduling components with explicit invariants and replaceable policies. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a policy-driven scheduling component library with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain classes as prototype-based constructs with strict bodies, constructors, methods, and fields.
Use private fields, static fields, and static blocks without confusing instance and constructor state.
Use accessors for stable property semantics without hiding expensive work or surprising side effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
